analyst_observations: Iteration 2 improved from 96.7% → 97.8% with-skill pass rate (TDD fix landed — eval 1 now 15/15 vs 14/15 in iter 1)
The TDD red-green-refactor assertion now passes 100% with-skill (was 50% in iteration 1) — the strongest improvement from the skill revision
Multi-lens eval (eval 3) achieved perfect 18/18 with-skill, demonstrating the dynamic skill selection works across backend/frontend/infra
Without-skill baseline improved slightly on eval 2 (6→9) but this appears to be variance, not a trend
Most discriminating assertions: Health Score (100% vs 0%), Dependency Map (100% vs 0%), TDD Reference (100% vs 0%), What's Working Well (100% vs 17%)
Non-discriminating assertions: before/after code examples (100% vs 100%) — Claude produces these naturally

The multi-lens assertions (16-18) show 100% with-skill vs 100% without — Claude naturally reviews all layers of a full-stack project regardless of skill. The skill's value is in the structured format, not the coverage.

comparison_to_iteration_1.key_fix: TDD assertion now passes 100% (was 50%), Phase 2 checkpoint template more consistent
